revision.text:
[[en:Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:赤坂天王山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Akasaka Tennozan Kofun | image = [[File:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, enkei.jpg|280px]]
Distant view of the tumulus | alternate_name = Akasaka Tennozan No. 1 Kofun | Cluster = Akasaka Tennozan Kofuns | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|1.5|N|135|52|28.5|E|region:JP-29_map_size:10000_type:landmark|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = North side: 50.5m
South side: 43.2m
East side: 46.5m
West side: 47m | built = Late 6th century | burial chamber = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|qid=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}} | excavations = None | alternate_name = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}: "Tennozan Kofun" }} '''Akasaka Tennozan Kofun''' (赤坂天王山古墳, Akasaka Tennozan No. 1 Kofun) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The tumulus is part of the Akasaka Tennozan Kofuns and is Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} under the name "Tennozan Kofun." It is hypothesized to be the true burial site of the 32nd emperor, {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|qid=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}. == Overview == The tumulus is a large {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located on the tip of a ridge extending northwest from the {{ill|Mount Tonomine|ja|多武峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Tonomine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Tonomine|qq=}} range. Although the site has not been excavated, surveys and measurements of the mound, chamber, and sarcophagus have been conducted. No grave goods remain due to earlier looting, but the structure and style suggest construction during the late 6th to early 7th century. The site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} on December 25, 1954. Recent investigations include detailed measurements and 3D imaging of the chamber[https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180623/11l/k29/040/512000c "Detailed Survey Results of Akasaka Tennozan Kofun Reported After 80 Years"] (Mainichi Shimbun, June 23, 2018).. == area and burial == [[File:Akasaka_tennnouyama_kohun_aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Akasaka Tennozan Kofun
(1985)|qq=}}{{small|{{National Land Aerial Photography|qq=}}.|qq=}}]] The tumulus consists of three tiers. It has a square type, tapering slightly from north to south: - North: 50.5m - South: 43.2m - East: 46.5m - West: 47m The flat top of the mound measures approximately 10m per side. The slopes are covered with paving stones, but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been found. File:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kofun mound == burial Chamber == [[File:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the chamber|qq=}}]] The burial chamber is a {{ill|two-sleeved horizontal stone chamber|ja|両袖式横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-sleeved horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-sleeved horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. It measures over 15.3m in total length, with the {{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|lt=main chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}} approximately 3-3.2m wide and over 4.2m tall. The walls feature a three-layered structure of large stones, with small stones filling the gaps. The {{ill|Sendou|lt=corridor|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendou|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendou|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} leading to the chamber is 8.5m long, 1.8m wide, and approximately 2m tall, made primarily of granite. The main chamber walls show a corbeling technique, and the floor is paved with gravel. At the center of the main chamber is a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|刳抜式家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} carved from white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. Measuring 2.4m long, 1.7m wide, and 1.1m tall, the sarcophagus features horizontal rope-mount projections and a central square cutout on its lid for access. No burial items remain due to looting. Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|Chamber layout Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|Sarcophagus layout Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (toward the main chamber) == Possible Interred Individual == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that Emperor Sushun was assassinated and buried in the Kurahashi region. As no other nearby tumuli match this description, Akasaka Tennozan Kofun was considered his burial site until the current {{ill|Kurahashi Okanoe Mausoleum|ja|倉梯岡上陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi Okanoe Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi Okanoe Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was Historic site during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, approximately 1.7km southwest. Some historians and archaeologists continue to regard Akasaka Tennozan Kofun as the likely burial site of Emperor Sushun. == Nearby Tombs == The ridge where the tumulus is located also contains smaller mounds with {{ill|horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, typeing a burial cluster: * '''Akasaka Tennozan No. 2 Kofun''': A 25m square tumulus west of No. 1. * '''Akasaka Tennozan No. 3 Kofun''': Located north of No. 1, with a 10m chamber. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Dannozuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Jomei's mausoleum) and other tombs along the {{ill|Obaragawa River|ja|粟原川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaragawa River|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaragawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Tennozan Kofun''': Designated on December 25, 1954{{National Historic site Database|401|1993|Tennozan Kofun|qq=}}. == Access == - From {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|qid=Q865801|lt=JR Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}} and {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|qid=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}}, {{ill|Sakurai Station|en|Sakurai Station|qid=Q1205698|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|1=Sakurai Station}}: Take a Nara Kotsu bus to Shimookuchi, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Study of Akasaka Tennozan Kofuns - Survey Report|author=The Sakurai City Historic site Association|year=2018|publisher=The Sakurai City Historic site Association|ref=|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic site Database|401|1993|Tennozan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|version=103817362|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Hidden category}}}} [[ja:赤坂天王山古墳]] [[en:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun]] {{Commons category|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun}} {{Commons category|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun | native_name =赤坂天王山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Akasaka Tennōzan Kofunn | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.500417|N|135.874583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|1.5|N|135|52|28.5|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Tennōzan Kofun'''|天王山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kurahashi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1954.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1993|title=天王山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is only of several sites claiming to be the grave of {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|qid=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}. It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun'''|赤坂天王山古墳|}}. ==Overview== The Tennōzan Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located at the tip of a ridge extending northwest of Mount Tonomine, near the left bank of the Oubara River. The mound is built in three tiers. The shape of the mound is rhomboidal, with the northern side approximately 50.5 meters, the southern side 43.2 meters, the eastern side 46.5 meters, and the western side is about 47 meters. The top is a flat area about 10 meters on each side. The slopes of each step are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones, but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] consists of a passageway and an antechamber, with a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} placed in the center of the antechamber, slightly toward the back wall. The burial chamber is made of natural {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}}, and {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|qid=Q12537|lt=Corbel|1=Corbel}}s can be seen on the walls of the chamber. The floor is paved with gravel, and a blocking stone remains at the entrance to the passage. The total length is over 15.3 meters, and the burial chamber is about 3 to 3.2 meters wide and over 4.2 meters high. The back wall is made of a megalith over two meters high and two more megaliths about one meter high, stacked in three tiers. The left and right walls are also stacked in three tiers, with pebbles placed in the gaps. The sarcophagus is about 2.4 meters long, 1.7 meters wide, and 1.1 meters high. The lid of the sarcophagus, made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|qid=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}}, is shaped like a house, with rope protrusions on the front, back, left and right. The body is a hollowed-out box with a square carving with sides of 48 centimeters from the lid to the body, slightly left of the center, and a small window. There is also a small square hole drilled in the upper edge of the body facing the passage, possibly by grave robbers in antiquity. The tumulus has been surveyed, but has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but there do not appear to be any surviving [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s. However, the burial chamber structure and the style of the Sarcophagus suggest that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} Emperor Sushun (c.552 - c.592) was the only emperor of Japan known with certainty to have been assassinated. The details of this incident are given in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', which also states that his tomb was located in "Kurahashi". However, in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' records state that the location of his tomb was unknown. However, [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family]] records gave the location as the temple of Konpuku-ji, located about 1.7 kilometers southwest, which was built on the site of Emperor Sushun's Kurahashi Shibagaki-no-miya palace, and which contains Emperor Sushun's ''[[:en:Ihai|Ihai]]'' memorial tablet. Until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the Tennōzan Kofun was regarded as the emperor's tomb: however, when the early Meiji period {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designated various ''kofun'' as "imperial graves", as small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|}}-style tumulus near the temple was arbitrary determined to be the "official" tomb of Emperor Sushun. Several noted archaeologists and scholars, including Koichi Mori, have asserted that the much larger Tennōzan Kofun has a far better claim to that title, although other candidates exist, including the [[Fujinoki Tomb]] in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|en|Ikaruga, Nara|qid=Q379233|lt=Ikaruga, Nara|1=Ikaruga, Nara}}. Akasaka_tennnouyama_kohun_aerial.jpg|Aerial photo Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Akasaka Tennozan Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Sarcophagus The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sakurai Station (Nara)|en|Sakurai Station (Nara)|qid=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|qid=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}}. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun}} *[https://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/material/files/group/54/akasakatennozan_Part1.pdf Sakurai City archaeological report] Akasaka Tennozan Kofun {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun]] {{Commons category|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun}} [[ja:赤坂天王山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11635409}} [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:崇峻天皇]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:incorrect template category applied]]
